thesis: GE Vernova is the most comprehensive 'picks and shovels' play in the global power infrastructure buildout. The combination of a record $163B backlog, accelerating AI data center demand, and expanding margins creates a compelling risk/reward profile. Q1 2026 results validated the bull case, with revenue and earnings beats, explosive order growth, and raised guidance.

financials.gross_margin: Expanding (adjusted EBITDA margin +390 bps in Q1 2026)
financials.fcf: Improving with margin expansion and backlog conversion

drivers: AI power demand sustains 20%+ order growth across gas turbines and grid solutions
Adjusted EBITDA margins expand 500+ bps as services mix increases and scale effects compound
Offshore wind project awards accelerate, particularly in U.S. and European markets
Multiple re-rates to growth premium as backlog visibility extends to 5+ years
Grid modernization mandates in U.S. and Europe drive sustained electrification segment growth

assumptions: Steady backlog conversion at current pace with 10–15% revenue growth
Margin expansion continues at ~200–300 bps annually
AI data center demand remains strong but moderates from peak growth rates
Wind segment delivers mixed results with offshore wins offsetting onshore pricing pressure
Services revenue grows in line with installed base expansion

risks: Order growth slows to single digits as AI data center capex cycle peaks
Wind segment drags on overall profitability due to project delays and cost overruns
Margin expansion stalls due to supply chain inflation or labor shortages
Multiple compresses to industrial average as growth decelerates
Geopolitical or regulatory headwinds delay international project execution

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — expect continued order momentum and potential guidance raise
Offshore wind project awards (U.S. lease rounds, European FIDs)
AI data center power partnership announcements (hyperscaler campus expansions)
Q3 2026 Earnings — backlog conversion trajectory and FY2026 guidance updates

key_risks: Execution risk on $163B backlog: supply chain constraints, labor shortages, or project delays could push out revenue recognition
Wind segment volatility: offshore wind has faced headwinds globally including permitting delays and rising costs
Valuation sensitivity: at ~$1,110, GEV trades at a premium multiple; any slowdown could compress valuation quickly
Geopolitical & regulatory risk: tariffs, export controls, or foreign energy policy changes could impact international backlog
Services renewal risk: early retirements of gas turbines due to policy or economics could reduce long-term services opportunity
